cooperates.cooperate_desc: Description: "Alice works perfectly with tanks like Tigreal. His ability to group enemies together with his Ultimate allows Alice to teleport in and drain the life of multiple enemies at once, maximizing her sustain and damage."

counters.counter_desc: "Alice counters marksmen who have low mobility and high attack speed. By closing the gap with her first skill and using her Ultimate, she can easily out-sustain their damage and finish them off quickly."

dominants.dominant_desc: "In the early game, Alice is relatively weak and mana-hungry. Focus on clearing minion waves efficiently to collect Blood Ancestry orbs. These orbs are crucial as they permanently increase Alice's Max HP and Mana. Avoid aggressive trades until you reach level 4 and have your core items like Clock of Destiny. Use your first skill to poke and escape dangerous situations."

emblems.emblem_desc: "I chose the Custom Mage Emblem with Impure Rage to maximize Marcel's magic burst damage in the Mid Lane. The extra Adaptive Penetration helps in dealing significant damage early on, while Impure Rage provides additional burn damage and restores mana whenever skills hit enemies. This allows Marcel to stay in lane longer and poke enemies effectively during the laning phase."

equips.equip_desc: "This build focuses on maximizing Alice's sustainability and magic lifesteal. Clock of Destiny and Concentrated Energy are the core items to increase her HP and healing. Oracle further boosts her life-drain from her ultimate, while Dominance Ice is essential for mana and reducing the enemy's healing and attack speed. This setup makes Alice very difficult to kill in team fights."

data.recommend: "This guide provides the best lifesteal and sustain build for Alice. Follow these tips to master her stacking mechanics and dominate the mid-lane in Season 40."

spell.spell_desc: "I chose Retribution to allow Marcel to take the Jungle role effectively. This spell is essential for securing buffs, turtles, and lords, which helps Marcel scale faster and gain a gold advantage. Use it strategically to finish off jungle monsters and slow down enemies during ganks."

data.title: How to master the demon queen

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Once Alice sets up and hits her ult, Ixia could use her ult to deal massive AoE damage to opponents.
Due to Alice's Ult having a delay, pairing up with Tigreal is great to give her the opportunity for setup.

counters.counter_desc:
Diggie's Ultimate is a counter to almost all CC abilities, which makes it difficult for Alice to setup.

Diggie's bombs is also extremely potent and scales in damage for each time it hits an enemy, forcing you to adapt another item build, especially if you are the target in the early game.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Dominate your lane by making sure to activate your passive early so you can sustain and pressure your lane. Don't overextend or be too agressive since you want to get to Level 4 and help your jungler for Turtle fight.

Once level 4 and you are contesting for the turtle, your job is to zone out enemy heroes. Use your Ult before the turtle is in retribution range to immobilize and pressure enemy jungler.

During the mid game, if you are not sure what to do, just keep pushing your lane (if turtle is up, and you can reach it, help during turtle fight.) Else, help your team in other lanes, after clearing your lane. Your goal is for your team to take down turrets to control majority of the map. By this stage, you are already quite tanky.

By this stage, you are extremely hard to take down. 

You will now act as the primary or secondary tank of your team. Your goal is for your team to push or kill enemy heroes safely so you need to give vision or initiate. If you are not yet item locked, don't forget to farm and push still.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Choose the Tank emblem for to make her defensive.

Vitality, so she can hit a little harder and she can heal a little bit more since some of her spells scale with HP

Tenacity, to make it harder to take her down when she has low health

Brave Smite, for that extra heal when hitting enemy heroes. Brave smite also activates every time Alice's passive hits an enemy hero; no need to use your active spells to try an activate if ever.

equips.equip_desc:
As an easy guide reference, this will be the standard build.

Cursed Helmet and Oracle will be your core items, while the rest you can replace to adapt to your opponents.

hero.hero_overview: Alice is a Tank/Mage that deals continuous damage and heals herself infinitely if near heroes. This guide focuses on her role as an Exp Lane hero, which is to be the frontline of your team.
hero.hero_strength:
-Alice's passive is basically always on if you are near enemy heroes, which deals continuous damage and healing for herself
-She is hard to take down when using a defensive build
-Scales well to the late game
hero.hero_weakness:
-She requires some practice; Alice is not a burst hero and her continuous damage, while painful over the long run, requires positioning and timing.
-While she has an amazing Ultimate, it also requires practice.
-She is slightly dependent on getting an item or two to maximize her skills and to make her a tanky frontline

dominants.dominant_desc:
- Engage when shield is ready; absorb initial burst, then drain.  
- Weave skills and autos to maximize heal and cooldown reduction.  
- Peel for carries and control space rather than solo diving alone.  
- Save Flicker for combo entry or escape after enemy CC is used.  
- Adjust items: anti-heal vs sustain comps, MR vs AP-heavy teams, early armor vs AD threats.

emblems.emblem_desc: Tank Emblem with Brave Smite and Tenacity turns Alice into a durable initiator: HP-focused leveling, CDR mid-game, and Bulwark for clutch survivability—synergizes with sustain items and Flicker engages

equips.equip_desc: Multiple full tank builds for Alice that prioritize frontline durability, sustain, and teamfight initiation. Each build includes recommended items (buy order), emblem, battle spell, when to pick it, situational swaps, skill order, and short playtips.

hero.hero_overview:
Overview

Alice is a sustain mage-fighter who excels at close-range trades and continuous teamfight presence by draining enemy HP to heal herself. Best played in mid or side lanes and as a roamer in coordinated plays, she uses gap-closing mobility and area drain effects to initiate, stick to targets, and survive extended engagements. Core mechanics revolve around entering melee range to apply drains, using her shield and mobility to absorb burst, and timing ultimates to disrupt enemy formations and secure multi-target heals. Strengths: strong sustain, lane dominance in close trades, and powerful teamfight zoning. Weaknesses: reliant on close proximity, vulnerable to heavy crowd control and long-range poke, and requires good timing to avoid being focused down.
hero.hero_strength:
Sustain and Outlasting — strong self-heal from drain mechanics lets Alice win extended trades and stay longer in lane.  
- Close-range Pressure — excels in melee skirmishes and can bully opponents who must trade up close.  
- Teamfight Presence — AoE drain and disruption heals allies and zones enemies during clustered fights.  
- Initiation and Stickiness — gap-close and shielding allow reliable engages and follow-up on targets.  
- Scaling with Items — benefits noticeably from AP and cooldown items, increasing both damage and sustain.  
- Roam Value — effective at rotating into fights to swing small skirmishes thanks to sustain and crowd control
hero.hero_weakness:
Short Range Dependence — must be in melee range to maximize effects, making her vulnerable to poke and kiting.  
- Vulnerable to Heavy CC — long stuns, silences, or roots prevent drains and leave her exposed.  
- Burst Susceptibility — low natural defenses make her an easy target for high-burst assassins and mages.  
- Item and Level Dependent — weaker early game; needs core items and levels to reach full effectiveness.  
- Predictable Engages — relies on closing distance; skilled opponents can bait or collapse on her engages.  
- Poor vs Long-range Poke — struggles to reach or survive sustained poke comps without teammate peel.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker (engage/escape) or Purify (vs heavy CC).

cooperates.cooperate_desc: The ultimate skills of the two heroes can paralyze enemies for a long time, which will give the team a chance to eliminate them.

counters.counter_desc: The ultimate skills of the two heroes can paralyze enemies for a long time, which will give the team a chance to eliminate them.

dominants.dominant_desc: Here you must rely on a team. You must be the one to start fighting or roaming, and you must prevent the deaths of those causing damage to your team.

emblems.emblem_desc: So you can stay alive for a long time in group confrontations, so you give your team a chance to eliminate enemies.

equips.equip_desc: Simply so you can withstand the enemies' skills and not die easily.

spell.spell_desc: Because it reflects damage and reduces the damage you receive, which is very useful with skill 3.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: 🌟 They provide a large, hard Crowd Control (CC) window, allowing Alice to follow up with her S1-Ultimate combo to guarantee the immobilize on the entire clustered team.
🌟 Alice's healing is strong, but extra shields and HP recovery make her nearly unkillable once she's in the middle of a fight and her passive is active.

counters.counter_desc: ✨ Baxia's passive drastically reduces her healing.
✨ Alice builds HP/Defense. Karrie's passive deals True Damage that ignore those defenses, quickly melting her regardless of her sustain.

dominants.dominant_desc: 💥 Passive (Crimson Blood Banquet): Use any skill twice on any enemy (hero, minion, creep) to activate a persistent AoE damage and healing aura around you. Hitting enemy heroes refreshes its duration.

💥 Start with S2 (Blood Awe): Short CD and close range make it the easiest way to trigger your Passive for early clear/sustain.

💥 Play Safe Early: Early cooldown on S1 (Crimson Gleam) is long. Save S1 for escape or a sure-kill engage.

💥 Ultimate Set-Up: Throw S1 (Orb) towards the backline  then Activate Ultimate (Throne of Ruin) (Gain CC Immunity + Damage Reduction) and S1 (Blink). This delivers the Ult's AoE Immobilize precisely onto the squishy targets.

💥 Stay in the Fray: Your healing and damage come from maintaining your Passive. After initiating, stick to the enemy heroes to keep the aura active.
💥 Defensive Ult: Use the Ultimate's Charge Phase (CC Immunity and Damage Reduction) defensively to survive enemy burst or to bait out key enemy skills before delivering the final Immobilize slam.

emblems.emblem_desc:
👑 By boosting her Max HP (Vitality and HP-based items) and adding two powerful, HP-based healing talents (Festival of Blood and Brave Smite), you turn Alice into an unkillable frontline monster in extended team fights.

🏅 Vitality - More HP means more sustain and more damage, making it a highly efficient talent for her bruiser playstyle.

🏅 Festival of Blood - This is essential for converting her constant AoE damage from her Passive and skills into massive healing. It stacks perfectly with her aggressive playstyle in the frontline.

🏅 Brave Smite - Since the revamped Alice's healing now scales with her Max HP, this talent provides a huge burst of healing every time one of her skills damages an enemy hero. You trigger this with Skill 1, Skill 2, or the initial hit of her Ultimate. This is arguably the best sustain talent for a high-HP Alice build.
emblems.emblem_title: HP Scaling and Pure Sustain Focus

equips.equip_desc:
🔥 Tough Boots - A standard, essential choice for a front-liner. Alice needs to charge her Ultimate and stay in the fight; reducing the time she is Stunned or Slowed is crucial for her survivability and damage output.

🔥 Cursed Helmet - A powerful item for Alice. It gives a massive amount of HP (which scales her Passive's heal and her Ultimate's damage) and the burn damage passive stacks with her own AoE Passive, helping her clear waves and constantly damage enemy heroes.

🔥 Oracle - This is a core item. The huge boost to HP is great, and the Bless passive synergizes perfectly with all of her healing sources: her Passive's heal, any spell vamp from emblems, and the healing from Queen's Wings and Brave Smite (if used).

🔥 Brute Force Breastplate - Another core item for Alice. Alice is constantly using skills and being hit, which quickly stacks the passive, giving her much-needed Movement Speed to chase or escape, along with extra Physical and Magic Defense.

🔥 Queen's Wings - Excellent late-game survival. It provides HP, more CDR, and the passive gives her a huge survivability spike (damage reduction) when her HP is low, allowing her to get a massive burst of healing from her full sustain kit (Passive, Oracle, Festival of Blood, Brave Smite) to turn the fight around.

🔥 Twilight Armor - A situational but strong defensive item. The cap on burst damage is fantastic for mitigating burst type heroes. Most importantly, it gives a huge HP boost, further scaling her damage and healing.

hero.hero_overview:
👑 Background & Identity

🌟 Queen of Blood.

🌟 Focused on a high-risk, high-reward dive and sustained combat style. Her core issue (Mana dependency) has been removed, but her sustain is now heavily linked to hitting enemies.

👑 Role & Specialty

🌟 Primary Role: Tank (due to high sustain and durability).

🌟 Secondary Role: Mage

🌟 Lane: Most commonly played in the EXP Lane or Jungle.

🌟 Specialties: Charge (mobility/initiation) and Regen (high self-sustain).

🌟 Playstyle: A late-game hero who excels in prolonged team fights by dealing continuous Area of Effect (AoE) damage while constantly regenerating her own HP.

👑 Skill Changes

🌟 Skill 1: Crimson Gleam (Mobility)

✨ Function: Fires a blood orb in a line, dealing damage. Recast to Blink/Teleport to the orb's location.

💫 Change: Cooldown is longer, making her mobility less spammable and more critical to use for committed engages or crucial escapes.

🌟 Skill 2: Doom Waltz (CC/Sustain Burst)

✨ Function: Deals AoE Magic Damage to nearby enemies and briefly slows them.

💫 New Mechanic: If Blood Banquet (Passive) is active, S2 scorches enemies, dealing an additional burst of Passive damage (3x Passivedamage/heal effect) and instantly healing Alice for the same amount.

💫 Use: This is the primary skill for immediate, high-sustain, and burst damage when the passive is already active.

🌟 Ultimate: Throne of Ruin (Initiation/Hard CC)

✨ Function: Alice gains Control Immunity and significant Damage Reduction but is slowed. After a brief channel/charge (approx. 1.5s), she slams down, dealing heavy AoE Magic Damage and Immobilizing all enemies in the area.

💫 Change: The Ultimate is no longer the perpetual life-drain ability (which is now her Passive). It is now a powerful, high-risk set-up and damage-mitigation tool with a long cooldown.
hero.hero_strength:
👑 Extremely durable in a committed fight, high AoE Max HP damage for shredding tanks, and a powerful team-fight initiation/counter-set with her ultimate.

👑 Combo (Initiation):

🔥 S1 (Crimson Gleam): Shoot toward the enemy backline.

🔥 Ultimate (Throne of Ruin): Immediately activate during the S1 travel.

🔥 S1 (Recast Blink): Teleport in.

🔥 Ultimate (Slam): Land for AoE Damage and Immobilize.

🔥 S2 (Doom Waltz): Trigger the massive burst sustain/damage from the active passive.
hero.hero_weakness: 👑 Significantly weaker early game. The long cooldowns on S1 and the Ultimate make her vulnerable if she commits without a follow-up or misses her S1 blink. Her sustain is no longer passive and relies on hitting enemies with skills.

data.recommend: Master the Queen of Blood using this Guide.

spell.spell_desc: 👑 Excellent for a full Tank-Mage build. Paired with her Ultimate's Damage Reduction, Vengeance makes her nearly immortal during a committed dive, maximizing her sustained Passive drain.

data.title: REVAMPED QUEEN OF BLOOD - ALICE

item.title: Kalea

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Carmilla's Crimson Flower Ultimate (CC & Defense reduction) combines perfectly with Alice's AoE Ultimate set. The two "Blood Sisters" lock down and shred the enemy team together.
Tigreal provides the initial 5-man set. Alice can then follow up immediately with her own Throne of Ruin for a guaranteed second wave of CC, eliminating any chance of escape.

counters.counter_desc: Baxia's Passive severely reduces Alice's healing and shield, crippling her core sustain mechanic and making her an easy target.
Heroes that deal Max HP True Damage (Karrie) can easily out-damage Alice's sustain and kite out of her Blood Banquet AoE.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game (Lv 1-7): Focus on Stacks & Safety
Your S1 cooldown is long. Use S2 to clear waves and poke the enemy laner to build Passive stacks. Avoid over-extending until you get CoD and Tough Boots. Max Skill 2 first for better wave clear and damage.

Mid Game (Lv 8-12): Roam & Initiate.
This is your power spike. Look for opportunities to rotate to the turtle/mid lane. Use the S1-Ult-S1 Blink Combo to catch opponents off-guard or to lock down the enemy carry for an easy pick-off. You are now tanky enough to sustain the initial burst.

Late Game (Lv 13+): The Team Fight Queen
Your role is the primary Initiator. Wait for a crucial moment, then execute the full combo. You can reliably immobilize 2-5 heroes, forcing a team fight win or creating space for your damage dealers to clean up. Do not enter the fight without your Ultimate ready.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Prioritize durability and healing/HP scaling.

Vitality (HP): A flat HP increase to boost her early survivability and her Passive's potential healing.

Tenacity: A solid choice in the revamped emblem system for Alice mains who play her as an initiator or a durable EXP Laner/Jungler.

Brave Smite: Restores HP when skills deal damage to an enemy hero, which perfectly synergizes with the constant ticking of her Passive and S2.

Change the Tenacity to Seasoned Hunter if you want to use Alice as Jungle.

This is why:
Seasoned Hunter: Great for EXP/Jungle Alice to clear objectives faster and gain a gold/level lead. Tenacity is a good alternative for late-game defense.

equips.equip_desc:
1. Tough Boots
Reduces CC duration, which is crucial for a melee initiator. Get Arcane Boots for more damage if CC is low.

2. Clock of Destiny (CoD)
Provides Max HP and Magic Power on stack, and a unique passive for more Max HP on level up. This item remains a staple for the necessary scaling.

3. Concentrated Energy
Provides massive Spell Vamp and a large Magic Power boost. This is her primary source of in-fight sustain.

4. Oracle
Core Item. Boosts all received healing and shields by 30%, amplifying the HP regen from her Passive and the Spell Vamp from Concentrated Energy.

5. Glowing Wand
Essential for sustained Magic Penetration and applying a constant Burn damage effect, which synergizes with the tick damage of her Passive.

6. Brute Force Breastplate
Excellent alternative for more durability, stacking Movement Speed, and Physical Defense when dealing continuous damage.

hero.hero_overview:
The Revamped Alice is a durable Magic Damage dealer that transforms from a vulnerable early-game hero into a devastating mid-to-late-game team fighter. Her core mechanic has shifted from mana management to stack management to activate her vital sustain.

Revamped Skills Overview: 

Passive: Crimson Blood Banquet
Stack Mechanic: When Alice hits enemies with skills, she gains stacks of Crimson (up to 4, 2 per hero hit). Sustain Zone: Once max stacks are reached, Blood Banquet is activated around her (her old Ultimate). This zone continuously deals Magic Damage (scaling with Magic Power and Max HP of the target) and restores Alice's HP. Hitting enemy heroes refreshes the duration of this effect.

Skill 1 : Crimson Gleam
Unchanged Mobility: Fires a blood orb forward. Can be recast to blink to the orb's location. This is still her primary tool for initiation and escape.

Skill 2:Doom Waltz
Damage & Buff Amplifier: Deals Magic Damage to enemies in a small area and briefly Slows them. If the Passive Blood Banquet is active, this skill deals an additional scorch hit, which amplifies the damage of the Blood Banquet while also restoring HP to Alice in equal measure.

Ultimate: Throne of Ruin
New Initiation & CC: Alice surrounds herself with blood, gaining Control Immunity and significant Damage Reduction while being slowed. After a short charge-up, she slams down, dealing heavy Magic Damage in a large area and Immobilizing all enemies hit for a short duration.
hero.hero_strength:
Massive Team Fight Control
The combination of S1 (blink) + Ultimate (Immobilize) provides a powerful, sudden, and reliable team fight initiation.

High Sustained Damage
Her Passive's damage scales with enemy Max HP, making her a nightmare for enemy tanks and fighters who stack HP.

Inevitable Initiation
Her Ultimate grants Control Immunity and Damage Reduction while charging, making it nearly impossible to stop once she commits, and guaranteeing the powerful AoE Immobilize.

Mana-Free Spells
The removal of mana cost allows her to spam skills without relying on Blue Buff or mana items (though CoD is still popular for its stats).
hero.hero_weakness:
Vulnerable Early Game
Her Skill 1 cooldown is very long early on, making her vulnerable to ganks and difficult to manage the lane.

Extremely Anti-Heal Dependent
Her entire survivability is tied to her healing from her Passive and Spell Vamp. Anti-heal items (Dominance Ice, NoD, Glowing Wand) cut her effectiveness in half.

Ultimate is Interruptible (During Charge)
Although immune to CC, certain suppress or knock-up effects (e.g., Franco's Ult, pre-charge Kaja's Ult) can disrupt the charge if not timed perfectly.

Highly Reliant on Items & Levels
She needs to complete her core items (CoD, Oracle) and reach a high level to properly transition into a durable damage dealer.

data.recommend: The revamped Alice has shed her old identity to become a formidable Initiator with unparalleled team fight control and sustained damage. Here is the complete guide for the Revamped Alice.

spell.spell_desc: The ultimate insurance policy. Use it for a sudden initiation after S1, or to escape a fight if her S1 is on cooldown.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: - Complementary Abilities: Heroes with abilities that enhance or support each other create strong synergies. This could involve one hero providing buffs while another deals damage, or one hero crowd-controlling enemies while another eliminates them.

counters.counter_desc: - Ability Counters: Some heroes have abilities that directly counter the abilities of others. For example, a hero with strong crowd control might counter a highly mobile but squishy hero.

dominants.dominant_desc: Alice is a mage hero known for her high HP regeneration, durability, blink capability, and crowd control skills. A good understanding of the map is essential to maximize her potential .
dominants.dominant_title: - ALICE experiment at CERN : This is a scientific experiment and not a character .

emblems.emblem_desc:
- Increased Magic Power: Alice is a mage hero, so the Mage Emblem can increase her magic power, enhancing the damage output of her skills.

- Magic Penetration: This emblem can also provide magic penetration, allowing her skills to deal more damage to enemies with magic resistance.

- Cooldown Reduction: Some Mage Emblem talents can reduce the cooldown of her skills, allowing her to use them more frequently.

- Mana Regeneration: Mana regeneration talents can help alleviate Alice's mana issues, allowing her to sustain her skills for longer periods.

equips.equip_desc: - Increased Damage: Alice's primary damage-dealing skills, such as Flowing Blood and Blood Ode, scale significantly with magic power. A magic build boosts the damage output of these skills, allowing her to quickly eliminate enemies

hero.hero_overview: Overview: Alice is a Mage/Tank hero known for her charge/regen capabilities. She can be a strong pick for both the EXP lane and mid lane .
hero.hero_strength:
- Solo Lane Potential: Alice can be left to roam alone without needing a healer, thanks to her ultimate, which allows her to sustain by sucking HP from creeps and enemy heroes .

- Lane Bully: She can be a nightmare for heroes who need to farm, disrupting their farming with her ultimate .

- Solo Push: Alice is capable of solo pushing lanes, using her Skill 1 to keep enemies away and Skill 2 to spam when enemies approach .

- Early Game Dominance: Alice excels in the early game, especially when farming for buffs. She can harass opponents with her skills .

- Team Support: Alice can help teammates in need by using her ultimate and Skill 1 to dislodge enemies .

- Jungle Control: Alice can invade the enemy jungle, stealing kills and buffs with her ultimate .

- High Damage: Alice's skillset can deal significant damage, especially with her passive skill, which adds HP, mana, and reduces cooldowns .

- Effective Clear Lane: Alice is effective at clearing minion waves, especially with Skill 1, which has a long attack range .

- Good Initiator: Alice is a good initiator, is extremely tanky in the late game, and can harass well in lane
hero.hero_weakness:
- Basic Attack: Alice's basic attack deals minimal damage, making her reliant on mana and skill usage .

- Mana Dependent: Alice is very mana-hungry, requiring mana buffs and items to sustain her skills .

- Limited Skill Spam: Due to her mana issues, Alice can't spam skills frequently and relies on cooldown and mana buffs .

- Team Compatibility: Alice may not be the best fit for fast-paced, agile teams, as she tends to wander and solo push .

- Escape Issues: Alice can struggle to escape from opponents, especially without mana to activate her passive skill for movement speed .

- Ultimate Dependency: Alice is vulnerable when her ultimate is on cooldown

data.recommend: - Strategic Gameplay Tips: Master the art of lane control, efficient jungling, and impactful team fight initiation with Alice's unique skill set.

spell.spell_desc:
- Objective Control: Retribution allows a hero to quickly secure jungle objectives like the Turtle and Lord. If a player wants to prioritize objective control and secure these buffs for their team, Retribution could be a viable option.

- Jungle Farming: Retribution can speed up jungle farming, allowing Alice to acquire gold and experience more quickly. This can be useful if the player wants to play Alice as a jungler.

- Invading: Alice can use Retribution to invade the enemy jungle and steal buffs, disrupting the enemy jungler's farm.

- Securing Kills: Retribution can be used to secure kills on low-health enemies, especially in the early gam

data.title: Alice guide for beginner to win

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Alice synergizes exceptionally well with Khufra, especially in coordinated teamfights. Khufra's ability to initiate and group enemies using his Tyrant's Revenge and Tyrant's Rage sets up the perfect stage for Alice's AoE-heavy kit. When Khufra dives in and knocks multiple enemies together, it creates a clustered zone where Alice can blink in with Skill 1 and unleash her ultimate for maximum impact. His crowd control not only locks enemies in place but also buys Alice the time she needs to activate Blood Banquet and sustain through damage. Together, they form a deadly combo: Khufra disrupts and compresses the enemy formation, while Alice follows up with healing, damage, and immobilization, turning chaotic fights into clean wipes.

Alice works extremely well with Atlas in teamfights because Atlas can force enemies to group together using his ultimate, Fatal Links. When Atlas pulls multiple enemies into one spot and stuns them, it creates the perfect setup for Alice to blink in with Skill 1 and unleash her area damage and crowd control. This combo allows Alice to fully activate her passive and deal maximum damage while healing through the fight. Atlas also provides strong frontline control, giving Alice the space she needs to enter safely and stay alive. Together, Atlas initiates the fight and locks enemies down, while Alice follows up with burst, healing, and immobilization to finish the job.

counters.counter_desc:
Baxia is one of the most effective counters to Alice revamped, especially when played as a roamer or jungler. His passive, 
Baxia Mark, reduces all healing received by enemies he damages—including spell vamp, item-based healing, and Alice's Blood Banquet passive. This directly undermines her core strength: sustained healing in extended fights. Every time Baxia hits Alice with skills or basic attacks, her ability to recover HP is significantly weakened, making her much easier to burst down or zone out of teamfights. His mobility also allows him to chase her down or disrupt her setup before she can activate her ultimate. In short, Baxia doesn't just slow Alice's snowball—he actively dismantles her sustain-based playstyle.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Alice's laning phase is inherently patient and defensive, but mastery of movement, skill timing, and stacking opportunities will reward players with a powerful mid and late-game impact. Here's an in-depth guide to early strategy, positioning, and transitioning to the midgame.

Early Game Positioning tricks 

Exp lane (typical use)

- Passive Playstyle: In the opening minutes, focus on last-hitting minions from a safe distance using Skill 1 (Crimson Gleam) for poke and zone control. Against strong poke matchups like Harith, Yu Zhong, or Esmeralda, stay near your tower and avoid unnecessary trade.

- Vision and Anti-Gank: Use Crimson Gleam into jungle entrances and bushes to check for incoming ganks. Always keep your teleport ready as an escape route. Do not overextend without vision, especially before level 4 and your first item.

Mid Lane (Rare, but Possible)

- Roaming Potential: After clearing the wave, Alice's map mobility allows quick roams to side lanes using her Skill 1 blink. Rotate early to pressure Gold or EXP lane. Roaming with your jungler at level 4 can secure objectives or kills using your crowd control.

Tips

- Engage Late: In chaotic teamfights or turret defenses, enter after the initial crowd control. Aim to catch 2 or more enemies with your ultimate after blinking in.

- Extending Combos: Use Skill 1 over walls or across the river to extend pressure or escape. Practice aiming to maximize map mobility and quick flanks.

- Stack and Sustain: Stay in fights as long as your passive is active and enemies are nearby. If your healing is reduced or passive is on cooldown, back off and reset before re-engaging.

emblems.emblem_desc: The emblem set was chosen to maximize Alice's sustain, mobility, and damage efficiency in extended fights. The Tank main emblem provides bonus HP and cooldown reduction, helping her survive longer and cast skills more frequently. Agility increases her movement speed, allowing smoother rotations, better chase potential, and easier repositioning during fights. Festival of Blood grants spell vamp with each kill or assist, which stacks up and boosts her healing - perfect for her AoE-heavy playstyle. Impure Rage adds bonus magic damage based on the enemy's max HP and restores mana with each skill hit, keeping her pressure consistent and her mana pool stable. This setup turns Alice into a durable, mobile, and self-sustaining mage who thrives in chaotic teamfights and objective control.

Mage as the main emblem boosts magic power and cooldown reduction so she can deal higher damage and cast more often; Thrill adds early magic penetration and damage for stronger trade and lane pressure; Festival of Blood provides stacking spell vamp to amplify her passive healing and make her hard to burst in teamfights; Impure Rage adds percent max HP magic damage and mana sustain so she can threaten tanks and keep spamming skills. Together these choices maximize damage, sustain, and pressure against both squishies and tanks.

equips.equip_desc:
This build for revamped Alice is designed to maximize her sustain, durability, and scaling damage across all phases of the match:

- Tough Boots: These reduce crowd control duration and give magic defense, helping Alice survive early poke and control effects while she stacks her passive.

- Clock of Destiny: A core scaling item that boosts HP, mana, and magic power over time. It synergizes perfectly with Alice's need for durability and late-game power.

- Oracle: Enhances Alice's healing effects from her passive and Concentrated Energy (if taken later), while also providing magic defense and cooldown reduction for more frequent skill usage.

- Brute Force Breastplate: Adds physical defense and movement speed with each skill or basic attack, giving Alice extra mobility and tankiness during extended fights.

- Glowing Wand: Applies burn damage over time, which complements her area skills and helps pressure enemies with sustained magic damage—especially useful against high HP targets.

- Queen's Wings: Offers a clutch survival tool by reducing incoming damage when Alice drops low, while boosting spell vamp to help her recover quickly and stay in the fight longer.

Together, this build balances survivability, scaling, and sustained damage—allowing Alice to thrive in long teamfights and punish grouped enemies while remaining hard to burst down.

hero.hero_overview: Alice revamped is a sustain-heavy mage who thrives in extended teamfights and objective control. Her new passive, Blood Banquet, builds up Crimson stacks through skill hits, which convert into healing and percentage max HP damage when active. This makes her increasingly durable and threatening as fights drag on. Her Skill 1 is a forward projectile that can be recast to blink, giving her flexible mobility for offense or escape. Skill 2 creates a damaging area that scales with her passive, allowing her to zone enemies and recover health. Her ultimate grants control immunity and damage reduction for a short duration, followed by a delayed AoE burst and immobilize—perfect for punishing grouped enemies and anchoring team engagements. Alice's revamped kit rewards smart positioning, timing, and stack management, making her a powerful pick for teams that want sustained pressure and zone control.
hero.hero_strength: Alice revamped shines in extended teamfights and objective control thanks to her powerful sustain and area damage. Her passive, Blood Banquet, allows her to heal and deal percentage max HP damage when fully stacked, making her incredibly durable in drawn-out engagements. Her ultimate grants control immunity and damage reduction, letting her anchor fights and survive burst attempts while punishing grouped enemies with delayed crowd control and AoE damage. She excels at zoning enemies during Turtle and Lord contests, forcing unfavorable positioning and creating space for her team. Once she has her core items and passive stacks, Alice becomes a frontline mage who can absorb pressure, disrupt formations, and turn chaotic fights into winning trades.
hero.hero_weakness:
Alice revamped has several key weaknesses that players should watch out for:

- Early vulnerability: She relies on stacking her passive to become effective, making her weak in the early game and prone to being bullied in lane.
- Limited mobility: Without her Skill 1 recast or Flicker, she struggles to escape dives or reposition during fights.
- Susceptible to burst: High-damage assassins or mages can eliminate her before she activates her sustain tools.
- Spread-out enemies: Her AoE abilities are most effective against grouped targets; teams that stay spread reduce her impact.
- Reliant on timing: Her ultimate has a delay before its crowd control lands, which can be dodged or interrupted if poorly timed.

These weaknesses make her dependent on good positioning, team coordination, and item progression to reach her full potential.

data.recommend: I super recommend this guide! Especially, for beginners.

spell.spell_desc: Provides crucial instant repositioning, whether for risky initiations, urgent escapes, or repositioning after using Crimson Gleam. It compensates for Alice's lack of dash or immunity outside of her Skill 1, making it a powerful defensive (or even surprise offensive) tool. Flicker is ideal for situations where the enemy has heavy burst/crowd control or if you need to cross terrain quickly.

spell.spell_desc: Sustainability

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Alice works best with heroes who can lock down enemies so she can deal sustained AoE damage. Tanks like Tigreal, Atlas, or Khufra can group enemies together, making it easier for her to use her skills and lifesteal. She also pairs well with supports like Rafaela or Estes who give healing and movement speed, keeping her alive longer. In return, Alice provides strong area control in team fights, punishing enemies that stay too close together.

counters.counter_desc: Hanabi is an easy target for Alice because she has no dash or escape skills. Alice can dive directly into her, ignore Hanabi lifesteal with her own stronger sustain, and burst her down in team fights. Since Hanabi relies on staying safe at the backline, Alice mobility and AoE lifesteal give her a huge advantage.

dominants.dominant_desc: Reason: Alice scales very well with items like Clock of Destiny and gains stronger lifesteal and sustain the longer the match goes. In late-game team fights, she can dive into enemies, heal massively, and deal continuous AoE damage, making her very hard to stop.

emblems.emblem_desc: I use Thrill, Inspire, and Lethal Ignition for Alice because this setup gives her the best balance of damage and sustain. Thrill adds magic penetration so her skills can pierce enemy defenses, Inspire gives cooldown reduction so she can spam her abilities more often, and Lethal Ignition provides extra magic damage that burns enemies when her skills hit. This combination makes Alice more effective in team fights, letting her deal consistent damage while healing from her lifesteal.

equips.equip_desc: This build balances Alice damage and durability, giving her strong magic power while staying tanky in team fights.

hero.hero_overview: Alice is a mage hero with strong sustain and crowd control. After her revamp, she became more powerful in team fights because of her lifesteal and area damage. She is usually played in the EXP lane or mid lane. Alice can dive into enemies, control team fights with her skills, and survive longer thanks to her regeneration. She is very dangerous in the late game when she already has her core items.
hero.hero_strength:
Strong Sustain: Excellent lifesteal, especially in team fights.

Durable Mage: Can deal high damage while being tanky at the same time.

Good Crowd Control: Has stuns and slows that disrupt enemies.

Team Fight Monster: Excels in the late game with her ultimate that can damage multiple enemies.

Initiator: Can dive into the backline and start fights with blink + AoE skills.

Scaling Power: Becomes stronger the longer the game goes on.
hero.hero_weakness:
Early Game Weak: Struggles before she gets items for sustain and cooldown.

Mana Hungry: Needs proper mana management in the early game.

Vulnerable to Burst: Can be killed quickly by heroes with high burst damage if caught off guard.

Crowd Control Sensitive: Stuns and silences can stop her lifesteal and regeneration.

Item Dependent: Needs core items to be effective (e.g., Clock of Destiny, Enchanted Talisman, and other sustain items).

data.recommend: This is just my suggestion for new Alice revamped but this is still about enemy side, those items can be changed by your own opinion. I HOPE THIS HELPS YOU AND OTHERS. THANK YOU

spell.spell_desc: Vengeance is a good spell for Alice because it lessens the damage she receives and reflects part of it back to enemies. Since Alice usually dives into team fights, this spell helps her survive longer while punishing opponents who attack her.

hero.hero_overview: Alice a prominent figure often called the Queen of Blood and is depicted as conyrolling the abyss and its blood demons through desire and power.
hero.hero_strength:
High Burst & AOE Damage: Her revamped skills allow for increased burst and area-of-effect damage, making her very effective in team fights. 
Sustained Combat & Healing: Alice's passive, Blood Ode, provides continuous healing and damage when activated, making her very hard to kill in prolonged fights. 
Mobility: Her Skill 1 provides a flexible dash, allowing her to maneuver easily across the battlefield and engage or disengage from fights. 
Objective Control: Her control immunity during her ultimate and passive activation makes her excellent for securing objectives like the Turtle and Lord in the jungle or during team fights. 
Flexibility: Alice can be built as a full damage dealer to eliminate squishy targets or as a semi-tank to contribute significant damage while being more durable.
hero.hero_weakness: Being gapped in early is a really big disadvantage for Alice mostlikely when the enemy's EXP lane uses Grock or Gloo because they counter all Alice's skills just like its first skill being countered by minsan and it make it hard for her to scape.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game Farming
Use Blood Ancestry (Passive) to heal and restore mana while farming.
Prioritize killing minions and absorbing blood orbs to quickly stack HP and magic power.
Skill Combo for Engagement
Crimson Gleam (1st Skill)  dash into enemies to initiate.
Blood Awe (2nd Skill)  AoE damage + immobilize and slow enemies.
Throne of Ruin (Ultimate)  activate during fights for control immunity, damage reduction, and sustain.
Positioning
Stay near your frontline or allies before engaging.
Use Crimson Gleam to reposition for maximum AoE effect or escape if needed.
Orb Collection
Always pick up blood orbs dropped by enemies and minions to maintain sustain and boost damage.
Team Fight Tips
Wait for enemies to clump before using Blood Awe.
Enter fights after your team initiates for maximum effect.
Use Ultimate during enemy ultimates or burst damage moments to survive longer.
Itemization Flow
Core items: warrior boots, clock of destiny, ice queen wand,
Twilight armor, queen wings, and lastly brute force
Adjust situationally depending on the enemy team.
Jungle Efficiency (if playing jungle)
Start with Retribution.
Farm efficiently using 1st skill to dash between camps.
Collect orbs while clearing jungle for fast scaling.

hero.hero_overview: Alice, once a formidable mage-tank hybrid, has been reimagined as a more team-oriented support hero. Her new abilities focus on crowd control, damage reduction, and sustain, making her a valuable asset in team fights. However, her solo carry potential has been diminished, and she now requires careful positioning and coordination with teammates to maximize her effectiveness.
hero.hero_strength:
Crowd Control (CC) Immunity: Her ultimate, "Throne of Ruin," provides control immunity and significant damage reduction, allowing her to initiate fights without being easily interrupted.

Sustain and Lifesteal: Abilities like "Doom Waltz" and "Blood Banquet" offer sustain through lifesteal, enabling her to heal during engagements.

Team Fight Presence: With her enhanced CC and durability, Alice can disrupt enemy formations and protect allies during team fights.
hero.hero_weakness:
Reduced Solo Carry Potential: Her damage output has been toned down, making her less effective at carrying games independently.

Vulnerability to Kiting: Alice's mobility is limited, and she can be easily kited by ranged or agile heroes, especially in the early game.

Dependence on Team Coordination: Her effectiveness heavily relies on team synergy, and poor coordination can lead to missed opportunities.

spell.spell_desc: Using retribution on alice is a great advantage this is because with alice first skill she can easily teleport into turtle or lord gaining advantage to steal the lord or turtle

cooperates.cooperate_desc: - Enhanced Survivability: Angela's ultimate, Heartguard, allows her to enter Alice and provide a shield and HP restoration. This significantly increases Alice's survivability, allowing her to stay in fights longer and absorb more damage.

counters.counter_desc: - Durability and Sustain: Alice is naturally tanky with high HP and can sustain herself with her ultimate (Blood Ode), which heals her based on the number of enemies nearby. Karrie relies on dealing damage over time, but Alice can often out-sustain Karrie's damage output, especially with items like Oracle.

dominants.dominant_desc: - Focus on Farming: Prioritize clearing minion waves and jungle camps to accumulate gold and experience.

emblems.emblem_desc: The Agility talent grants Alice a 4% increase in movement speed, which is useful for roaming between lanes, assisting teammates, and quickly getting into position for team fights.

Frequent Skill Usage: The Inspire talent grants a 5% increase in cooldown reduction, allowing Alice to use her skills more frequently. This is particularly useful in the EXP lane, where consistent damage and sustain are required.

equips.equip_desc:
Ice Hunter's Tough Boots:
- Jungle Item: The "Ice Hunter's" enchantment on the boots provides the Retribution battle spell with an enhanced effect (slows enemies and deals true damage), which is essential for a jungler to clear camps faster and secure objectives like Turtle and Lord.

- Passive (Scorch): Deals magic damage equal to 2% of the target's current HP per second for 3 seconds. This is an excellent item for Alice in the EXP lane, as it allows her to consistently apply damage over time to enemy heroes, especially tanky fighters, wearing them down during prolonged engagements.

hero.hero_overview: Blood Ancestry (Passive): When a nearby minion or creep dies, a blood orb forms. If an opposing hero or siege minion dies, the blood ball increases to two. Alice gains increased HP and mana each time she absorbs a blood
hero.hero_strength: Alice can use Skill 1 to deter opponents from approaching lanes. Her Skill 2 can be spammed, especially as she levels up, and her ultimate combined with Skill 2 can sustain her while pushing
hero.hero_weakness: Alice's mana supply limits how long she can use her skills. She is susceptible to running out of mana in a team fight if not managed correctly

spell.spell_desc: Retribution helps Alice secure jungle buffs like the Blue Sentinel and Red Buff, which provide crucial buffs to mana regeneration, damage, and slow effects. This is especially important in the early game.
